mnemonics.title: FIDEM - Fetch, Interpret, Decode, Execute, Memory
mnemonics.content: FIDEM: 'F'etch, 'I'nstruction 'D'ecode, 'E'xecute, 'M'emory - The 5 stages of basic instruction cycle in sequence

mnemonics.title: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away
mnemonics.content: Pipeline stages: P-ipeline, D-ecode, N-xt, T-hrow, S-ausage, P-izza, A-way (for 7-stage pipeline: Prefetch, Decode, Operand Fetch, Execute, Store Result, etc.)

mnemonics.title: IFDE - Instruction Fetch Decode Execute
mnemonics.content: IFDE: 'I'nstruction 'F'etch, 'D'ecode, 'E'xecute - The fundamental 3-step instruction cycle every CPU follows

mnemonics.title: SBC - Structural, Data, Control Hazards
mnemonics.content: SBC: 'S'tructural (hardware conflict), 'B'ranch/'D'ata hazards (resource conflicts), 'C'ontrol (branch misprediction) - Three types of pipeline hazards

mnemonics.title: FDEWWM - Fetch Decode Execute Write-back Write Memory

acronyms.usage: Holds address of next instruction to be fetched; increments after each fetch (PC+4 in MIPS)
Stores the currently fetched instruction being decoded and executed
Holds the memory address from which data will be fetched or to which data will be stored

acronyms.expansion: Memory Buffer Register / Memory Data Register
acronyms.usage: Temporarily holds data being transferred to/from memory
Performs arithmetic and logical operations during Execute phase of instruction cycle
Generates control signals to coordinate instruction cycle stages
Data hazard where later instruction reads data written by earlier instruction; solved by forwarding/stalling
Write-after-read hazard in out-of-order execution; later instruction writes before earlier instruction reads
Write-after-write hazard where instructions write to same destination in wrong order
Average clock cycles per instruction; ideal pipeline CPI = 1; calculate speedup = (non-pipelined CPI × pipeline stages) / (pipeline CPI + stalls)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a factory assembly line: each station (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory, Write-back) does one specific task on different instructions simultaneously, like workers on a car assembly line

Picture a water pipe with a bubble blocking flow - the bubble (stall cycle) stops all water (instructions) behind it until it passes; visualize empty slots moving through pipeline like empty train carriages
Think of a relay race - runner 2 (EX stage) passes baton directly to runner 3 (MEM stage) without waiting for previous runner to finish the full track (write-back); a shortcut path in the pipeline
Imagine guessing which road a friend will take at a fork in the path - if correct, continue smoothly; if wrong, must backtrack and take the other path (pipeline flush)
Structural hazard = one lane bridge (two cars can't pass simultaneously); Data hazard = one car needs output from another car ahead; Control hazard = road sign deciding which path to take

analogies.analogy: Cooking a recipe: Fetch = getting recipe card, Decode = reading ingredients/instructions, Execute = cooking, Memory = taking from pantry/storing to fridge, Write-back = serving the dish

Laundry washing: While machine 1 washes first load, machine 2 dries second load, machine 3 folds third load - multiple instructions in different stages simultaneously, not sequentially waiting for each to complete
Train station platforms - each platform (register) holds one passenger (instruction) between stations (stages); trains (instructions) keep moving, passengers board/exit at each platform
GPS navigation taking wrong exit on highway - must turn around (flush pipeline) and go back to correct exit; the wrong-path instructions executed during misprediction are wasted
Team project where Person B cannot start their section until Person A finishes their part - Person B must wait (stall) or Person A must pass incomplete work early (forwarding)
Single bathroom in a house - two people cannot use it simultaneously; must wait for one to finish before other can use it (hardware limitation)
